This research proposes a framework for synchronizing BPMN model artifacts and its natural language text representation.
This research proposes a framework for synchronizing BPMN model artifacts and its natural language text representation.
The framework receives a model as input, triggers the Process Model to Natural Language component, which queries the linked elements, and generates the whole text.
The framework receives a model as input, triggers the Process Model to Natural Language component, which queries the linked elements, and generates the whole text.
The framework was implemented using Java standard technology and evaluated through experiments.
The framework was implemented using Java standard technology and evaluated through experiments.
The Model to Text component of the framework was implemented following the Three-Step NLG pipeline proposed by Reiter and Dale, which are: Text Planning, Sentence Planning, Sentence Realization.
The first experiment's objective was Assess whether the knowledge represented by the generated process description can be considered equivalent to the process model.
74% of the subjects claim knowledge represented by the manually updated text is equivalent to the automatically updated process model.

answers.answer_rationale: The paper presents original research describing a novel approach for automated generation of BPMN models from text, including an implemented research prototype and evaluation on industry and textbook examples, indicating primary research.
supporting_quotes: Our contribution is a corresponding technique that does not make any assumptions about the structure of the provided text.

answers.answer_rationale: The study specifically covers use of NLP for process extraction from natural-language text by developing automated generation of BPMN business process models from natural language input, combining NLP tools and evaluating the results.
supporting_quotes: In this paper, we tackle this problem based on an automatic approach to generate BPMN models from natural language text.
We combine existing tools from natural language processing in an innovative way and augmented them with a suitable anaphora resolution mechanism.
The evaluation of our technique shows that for a set of 47 text-model pairs from industry and textbooks, we are able to generate on average 77% of the models correctly.

answers.answer_rationale: The paper explicates a concrete method by detailing the multi-phase transformation pipeline, including sentence level analysis, text level analysis, and model generation steps, supported by NLP tools such as the Stanford Parser, FrameNet, and WordNet, as well as a custom anaphora resolution algorithm.
supporting_quotes: The text is processed in several stages. First, a tokenization splits up the text into individual sentences.
Then, each sentence is parsed by the Stanford Parser.
Next, complex sentences are split into individual phrases.
An experimental evaluation... showed good accuracy of 63.06% for anaphora resolution.
The last phase of our approach the information contained in the World Model is transformed into its BPMN representation via a nine step procedure.

answers.answer_rationale: The paper describes an evaluation on a dataset of 47 text-model pairs from academic, industry, textbook, and public sector sources, measuring similarity via Graph Edit Distance, achieving on average 77% similarity, confirming empirical validation of the method.
supporting_quotes: For the validation of our approach, we collected a test data set consisting of 47 of those text-model pairs.
The evaluation results... the approach is able to correctly recreate 77% of the model in average.
The results are highly encouraging as only minor corrections by a human modeler are required.

title: Extracting business process entities and relations from text using pre-trained language models and in-context learning

In this paper we explore, for the first time in literature, the feasibility of using in-context learning over the pre-trained language models to perform process extraction from textual documents in a conversational fashion.
The experiments we have conducted provide two types of insights.
